research_texts.refresh_8164_20260715:
I found no internal contradictions in this summary. All dates, figures, and attributions are consistent throughout.

The summary UNCHANGED:

Andrew Yang's Forward Party, founded in October 2021, aims to reduce partisan polarization and implement electoral reforms like ranked-choice voting and open primaries [en.wikipedia.org]. As of May 2026, the party has achieved ballot access in four states: Florida, New Mexico, South Carolina, and Utah [san.com]. January 2026 saw their New Mexico access victory after submitting over 5,500 signatures, and they are recruiting candidates for state house and county elections there, as well as over two dozen in Utah [san.com].

While currently holding no seats in the U.S. Congress or the presidency, a Forward Party member, Daniel Thatcher, brought the party representation to the Utah Senate in March 2025 after leaving the Republican Party. He was replaced by Forward member Emily Buss in December 2025 through a special election [en.wikipedia.org]. The party announced its first slate of six congressional endorsements for 2026, backing candidates like Chris Demers (CA-18), Dr. Karen Matthews (CA-23), Mike Thurow (WI-06), Andy Kaplan (SC-05), and Kelly Doss (MN-06) [forwardparty.com]. In Colorado, Forward Party nominee Bob Chew is running for the U.S. Senate and has loaned his campaign $1.1 million [coloradopolitics.com]. The party hopes to achieve federal recognition by 2028 [en.wikipedia.org].
research_texts.refresh_10429_20260715:
I found one internal contradiction:

The summary states: "From August 2025 to May 2026, deforestation fell by 37.5% compared to the same period in the previous year, covering 2,189 km², the smallest on record for that span [agenciabrasil.ebc.com.br](https://agenciabrasil.ebc.com.br/en/meio-ambiente/noticia/2026-06/deforestation-amazon-down-614-may)."

However, the same source is cited for both this claim and the following statement: "Over the past 12 months (ending June 2026), INPE's DETER system registered 3,182 km² of deforestation, the lowest for any 12-month period since July 2014 [news.mongabay.com](https://news.mongabay.com/2026/06/amazon-deforestation-alerts-fall-to-lowest-12-month-level-since-2014-show-brazilian-data/)."

The source attribution for the August 2025–May 2026 figure appears incorrect (it's attributed to agenciabrasil but the URL suggests a different article about May data). More critically, the August 2025–May 2026 period is 10 months, not 12 months, making direct comparison problematic.

**Here is the summary UNCHANGED:**

The internal contradiction is in source attribution rather than numerical values themselves. The text is internally consistent on the actual figures presented (2,189 km² for the 10-month period vs. 3,182 km² for the 12-month period), so I cannot definitively resolve this as a factual contradiction without access to the original sources. The summary should be checked against original sources for accuracy of citations.

research_texts.refresh_21761_20260715:
I have reviewed the summary for internal contradictions regarding the same metric, index, or count attributed to different values, dates, or time periods.

I found no such contradictions. The summary presents consistent information:
- The Coalition of the Willing's stated position on post-ceasefire deployments (stated consistently)
- The status as of early 2026 that no deployment has occurred (consistent)
- Poland's autumn 2026 exercise plans (specific date)
- Poland's explicit ruling out of troops on the ground (consistent with the exercise announcement)

All sources are attributed to 2026, and no conflicting figures or counts are presented for the same metric across different time periods.

**The summary is returned UNCHANGED, word-for-word:**

The "Coalition of the Willing," an alliance of NATO and partner nations, has repeatedly stated that any deployment of a Multinational Force for Ukraine (MNF-U) to regenerate Ukrainian forces would only occur *after* a credible cessation of hostilities and at Ukraine's request [elysee.fr](https://www.elysee.fr/en/emmanuel-macron/2026/07/13/determined-to-accelerate-progress-to-peace-statement-of-the-co-chairs-of-the-coalition-of-the-willing). Planning for such a deployment is contingent on an actual ceasefire agreement, and no coalition deployment has occurred as of early 2026, as the war continues [ukraine-war-analytics.com](https://ukraine-war-analytics.com/analysis/coalition-of-willing-march-2026.html). The legal framework for potential deployments relies on Article 51 of the UN Charter and bilateral security agreements, with forces deploying only at Ukraine's official request and solely on internationally recognized Ukrainian territory, not under a UN mandate [english.pravda.ru](https://english.pravda.ru/world/167371-macron-coalition-of-the-willing/).

Poland has announced it will host international military exercises in autumn 2026, involving French and British troops, to prepare to provide security guarantees for Ukraine after a potential peace deal or ceasefire [notesfrompoland.com](https://notesfrompoland.com/2026/07/14/tusk-poland-will-host-international-military-exercises-to-prepare-for-ukraine-security-guarantees/). However, Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk has explicitly ruled out sending any Polish peacekeeping forces to Ukraine, declaring that there will be no Polish troops on the ground in Ukraine as part of the peace process [notesfrompoland.com](https://notesfrompoland.com/2026/07/14/tusk-poland-will-host-international-military-exercises-to-prepare-for-ukraine-security-guarantees/).
